title: 皐月賞の後に・2つの可能性

text_block_list.name: Caster
text_block_list.text: Fuji Kiseki takes first place!
Wooooo!

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text: Huff! Huff! I did it!

text_block_list.name: Spectator A
text_block_list.text:
U-unbelievable! Did you see how fast she was
going? Fuji Kiseki's a force to be reckoned with!

text_block_list.name: Spectator B
text_block_list.text:
Right?! She was so mesmerizing, I couldn't take
my eyes off her!

Fuji Kiseki's performance at the Satsuki Sho has
set the crowd ablaze!

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text: Thank you! Your support is what keeps me going!

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
But it's not time to wake up from this lovely
dream yet! I hope you're looking forward to
seeing me at the Japanese Derby!

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text: (I sure talked big back there, but...)

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text: Hm...

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text: The truth is, something's bothering me.

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
Would it be all right if I did some solo training
tonight to check out a few things?

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
You say your legs felt strange after completing
the Satsuki Sho, but it doesn't seem to be an
injury, correct?

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
Right, it's nothing like that. But I feel like I'm
being held back by some kind of wall, and I
wanted to discuss it with you.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
A wall, huh? In other words... you want to talk
about your distance aptitude, yes?

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text: Exactly. I knew you'd get it!

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
I've felt this way for a while now, but I think I'm
most suited to be a miler.

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
I'm sure I'll be able to get accurate data with your
assistance, so could you help me, please?

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Hmm, I could... But the thing is, whatever one
does, there must be an equivalent exchange.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
How's this? If you help test out my new medicine,
I'll lend you a hand in your research.

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text: Um... It's a deal! I'll take your medicine!

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Good! Glad we're on the same page. Now, let's
not waste any time getting started.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
But first, a few words of warning—even if you run
the same times during training as you do in a real
race, the stress on your body will differ.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Remember to take these test results with a grain
of salt. Right, let's start you off with a
2,400-meter run!

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text: Huff! Huff! Wheeze!

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
(This is the fourth corner—I have to burst into a
final spurt!)

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text: Nghhh! Come ooon!

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Oooh! Well, well, well! Gut instincts are truly
nothing to sneeze at.

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text: How was that?

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
What fascinating results! Ah, but I mustn't get
ahead of myself.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Are you familiar with the terms "aerobic energy"
and "anaerobic energy"?

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text: Of course! We learned about them in class.

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
If I remember correctly, we use aerobic energy to
maintain our cruising speed during races...

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
And we use anaerobic energy to accelerate
quickly at the beginning and end of a race. Did I
get that right?

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
You certainly did! During races, our bodies
generate these two types of energy and consume
them as we run!

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
They're both essential, but the way anaerobic
energy is used is particularly important.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
It's used during critical points in a race, but
there's only so much of it. Deplete it, and our legs
will stop working out of sheer exhaustion.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
In other words, much hinges on your ability to
preserve your anaerobic energy until the final
turning point.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
At the same time, if your body is unable to
maintain your running speed with aerobic energy,
it will supplement your muscles with anaerobic
energy.

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text: Hm, so that would mean...

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
The faster your pace and the longer the race, the
more your stores of anaerobic energy will get
used up, right?

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Roughly speaking, yes! Now, let's examine your
run.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
After the 2,000-meter mark, your form began to
deteriorate and you were visibly struggling to
keep your pace.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
I suspect this is because you had depleted your
stores of anaerobic energy while trying to sustain
your speed.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Then, right before the end, when you were just
about to reach the finish line and were trying to
speed up...

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text: Nghhh! Come ooon!

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
You were out of anaerobic energy and lactic acid
had built up in your legs, utterly exhausting you!

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Your strongest asset is your ability to put on an
extra burst of speed in the final stretch, but when
you tried to do it... Well, you ran out of gas.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Taking into consideration our findings, my
conclusion is that long-distance runs aren't your
forte.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
You are exceptionally good at converting
anaerobic energy into bursts of speed in
short-distance sprints...

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
However, your body's ability to transport oxygen
to your muscles, which affects the production of
aerobic energy, appears to be only average.

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text: I see... Everything you're saying makes sense.

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
Thanks so much for your help. Now I can finally
progress my training!

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
No problem at all. And now, it's time for my side
of the bargain! Time to take your medicine!

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
Is this actually safe to drink? The color is really...
alarming.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text: Don't sweat the small stuff! Bottoms up, now!
The next day, Fuji Kiseki stops by my office.

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
Yesterday, Tachyon helped me figure out what
race lengths I'm best suited for. Here's what we
discovered...

She goes on to explain how participating in
anything longer than a medium-distance race will
drastically decrease her chances of winning.

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
That's all to say that tackling the Japanese Derby
and Kikuka Sho will be more herculean tasks than
we anticipated.

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
Now that I realize this, I wanted to talk to you
about our plans for the future.

Winning the Japanese Derby and the Kikuka Sho
will be difficult since Fuji Kiseki isn't well suited
for those distances. Thus, I can only assume...
choice_data_list:
Do you want to give up on getting the Triple
Crown?

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text: No, not at all! In fact, I want to do the opposite!

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
I now have two dramatic paths to choose between
precisely because of my incompatibility with
long-distance races!

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
I can continue taking on the Triple Crown tour,
which will now be all the more interesting due to
how challenging it will be...

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
Or I can change directions completely and throw
everything I've got into conquering the mile tour!

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
Sorry for springing this on you, especially since
you've been helping me train for the Triple Crown
tour all this time.

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text: When I realized I was facing a wall I couldn't simply muscle through, I started thinking about what I could do instead.

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
Winning the Triple Crown is a huge honor, and
there would be no greater show I could put on
and have end in victory.

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
That's why I wanted to claim glory upon that
enormous stage and give everyone a truly
fulfilling sense of catharsis.

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
But if I'm more suited toward mile races, then that
changes things.

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
I could pursue a different path—I could push my
sprinting ability to the max and put on a fantastic
show on the mile tour!

text_block_list.name: Fuji Kiseki
text_block_list.text:
I know this is sudden, but what do you think I
should do? Which path should I take?

Just which path should we choose? In the end, I'm
unable to give her an answer right away.
